title: [Latest] Global Compostable Flexible Packaging Market Size/Share Worth USD 5.18 Billion by 2035 at a 12.3% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
author: Custom Market Insights
description: The global compostable flexible packaging market is projected to grow from USD 1.42 billion in 2025 to USD 5.18 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 12.3%. Growth is driven by strict regulations against single-use plastics, increasing consumer demand for sustainable packaging, expanding composting infrastructure, and innovations in high-barrier compostable materials. Key players include NatureWorks LLC, Novamont S.p.A., Danimer Scientific, TIPA Corp., and others.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Included as a key player in the compostable flexible packaging market experiencing robust growth driven by sustainability trends.
Listed as a major player in the compostable flexible packaging market, which is projected to grow significantly over the next decade.

title: Mono-Material Flexible Food Packaging Films Market Poised for Strong Growth, Projected to Reach USD 2.87 Billion by 2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global mono-material flexible food packaging films market is projected to grow from USD 1.6 billion in 2025 to USD 2.87 billion by 2035 at a 6.0% CAGR. Growth is driven by sustainability demands, regulatory pressure, and consumer preference for recyclable packaging. Europe leads the market, while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Major companies are investing in high-performance mono-material solutions to replace multi-layer laminates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Invested in high-performance production lines for AmPrima mono-PE films with proprietary technology matching multi-material performance, positioning as market leader in recyclable solutions.
Invested approximately $9 million in UK-based recycling facility for closed-loop mono-material film systems, supporting circular economy and sustainability goals.
Launched Cryovac VPP MonoPro recycle-ready mono-material VFFS pack, helping processors transition from complex laminates to sustainable single-material packaging.
Supplies high-performance polyolefin resins for MDO-PE structures with transparency and recyclability, supporting the growing demand for mono-material solutions.
Develops specialized polyethylene resins enabling high-barrier, recycle-ready flexible food pouches, positioning as key material supplier for market growth.
Features EnviroFlex line of mono-material polyethylene and polypropylene structures with tailored barrier properties, supporting market expansion.
Directed significant capital into specialized manufacturing for mono-PE and FunctionalBarrier solutions designed to replace aluminum-lined packaging, showing strategic market positioning.
Expanded global footprint with investments in blueloop technology for high-barrier mono-material PE and PP solutions, demonstrating growth in sustainable packaging segment.

title: This $6 Billion Materials Firm Has Decided To Go Private
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Sealed Air Corporation reported strong Q4 results with adjusted EPS of 77 cents beating estimates of 73 cents, and sales of $1.40 billion exceeding the consensus of $1.34 billion. The company agreed to be acquired by CD&R funds in an all-cash deal valuing it at $10.3 billion, with the transaction expected to close mid-2026. Post-closure, Sealed Air will be delisted from the NYSE and become privately held.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company beat earnings expectations on both EPS and revenue metrics, demonstrated revenue growth in key segments (Protective +3% YoY, Food +2% YoY), and improved adjusted EBITDA. The acquisition at $10.3 billion represents a premium exit for shareholders, though the delisting removes public market trading opportunities.

title: Aluminium-Free High-Barrier Films Market to Reach USD 3.82 Billion by 2035 as Sustainable Packaging Replaces Foil-Based Structures
author: Towards Packaging
description: The global aluminum-free high-barrier films market is projected to grow from USD 1.79 billion in 2026 to USD 3.82 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 8.75%. Growth is driven by sustainability regulations, demand for recyclable mono-material packaging, and brand commitments to reduce carbon footprint. Major companies are investing heavily in advanced coating technologies and production capacity to meet rising demand across food, pharmaceutical, and consumer goods sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Acquiring Sonoco's thermoformed and flexibles business for USD 1.8 billion to significantly boost global capacity for high-barrier films, indicating aggressive market expansion strategy.
Investing in new global production technologies for mono-material flexible packaging with retort-grade barrier properties, positioning itself as a key player in aluminum-free innovation.
AmLite line uses transparent silicon oxide coating for foil-level protection in metal-free, recyclable structures, demonstrating competitive positioning in growing aluminum-free market.
CRYOVAC barrier films use multi-layer polyolefin technology for superior oxygen and aroma protection without metal, positioning company well in sustainable packaging transition.

title: North American Yogurt, Cheese & Meat FFS Packaging Market is Poised for Strong Growth 2026 to 2035
author: Towards Packaging
description: The North America yogurt, cheese & meat Form-Fill-Seal (FFS) packaging market is expected to grow from USD 1.75 billion in 2025 to USD 2.54 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 3.8%. Growth is driven by rising demand for convenient, hygienic, and sustainable packaging solutions, with flexible FFS formats and polypropylene materials leading adoption across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is actively investing in high-barrier flexible films and recycle-ready flow wraps for North American meat and dairy industries, positioning itself well for market growth driven by sustainability trends.
Specializes in automated vacuum and vertical FFS systems with oxygen-scavenging technology for fresh proteins, addressing key market needs for extended shelf life and food safety.
Focused on high-speed FFS machinery and multilayer barrier films for high-volume dairy and vacuum-packed meats, directly aligned with market growth drivers and automation trends.
